title: María Clara
text: Betrothed of Crisóstomo Ibarra. Daughter of Cap. Tiago, raised in the convent. Her ring is one of the six relics.

title: Elías
text: A mysterious boatman who saved Ibarra's life. He carries the letter revealing the truth of Ibarra's father.

title: Fr. Dámaso
text: A cruel Franciscan friar and the true father of María Clara. Antagonist of Noli Me Tangere.

title: Cap. Tiago
text: A wealthy kapitan of San Diego and Clara's supposed father. He gives a doubloon to aid Ibarra's quest.

title: Sisa
text: A mother driven mad by the loss of her sons Basilio and Crispín. She brews a herbal remedy relic.

title: Crisóstomo Ibarra
text: The protagonist of Noli Me Tangere. Returns from Europe to reform his country, only to uncover tragedy.

title: Wooden Crucifix
text: A simple cross worn smooth by prayer. Hidden near the chapel.

title: Father's Letter
text: Sealed and stained — the truth of your lineage. Carried by Elías.

title: Clara's Ring
text: A band of promise, given in the convent garden.

title: Forbidden Tome
text: Banned by the friars — its pages whisper of reform.

title: Herbal Remedy
text: Sisa's brew — restores vitality in dark hours.

title: Doubloon
text: Cap. Tiago's gold — heavy with compromise.

title: San Diego
text: The lakeside town where Noli Me Tangere unfolds. Home to the plaza, the convent, and the lake.

title: The Convent
text: Where María Clara was raised, and where Sisa's sons were taken.

title: The Lake
text: Elías's domain. Many secrets lie beneath its waters.

title: Relics Restored
text: The six relics have been returned to María Clara. San Diego will remember this day.
